question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: NIP vs Liquid (BO3) - BLAST Open Rotterdam Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket quarterfinal 2 match between NIP and Liquid in the BLAST Open Rotterdam Group B, initially scheduled for March 21 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "NIP" if NIP win the match against Liquid.

This market will resolve to "Liquid" if Liquid win the match against NIP.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Liquid holds a strong edge in trader consensus for their BLAST Open Rotterdam Group B BO3 clash with Ninjas in Pyjamas, implied probabilities around 70% favoring Liquid based on their dominant 3-0 group stage run including wins over Eternal Fire and Complexity. NIP struggles with inconsistency, dropping a 0-2 to 3DMAX and showing map-specific weaknesses on Inferno and Nuke where Liquid thrives historically (5-2 h2h advantage). No roster changes or stand-ins reported; both squads at full strength per official HLTV lineups. NIP's upset potential hinges on REZ's AWPing and home-crowd energy in Rotterdam, but Liquid's firepower from NAF and Twistzz, plus rest advantage, sustains sentiment—watch vetoes for Mirage control.

question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 2 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit enters as heavy favorites against PARIVISION in their BLAST Open Rotterdam Group B BO3 matchup, with trader consensus reflecting Spirit's top-10 HLTV ranking and dominant recent form, including a 2-0 win over 9INE and strong showings at IEM Chengdu. PARIVISION, languishing outside the top 30, has struggled with inconsistency, dropping a 0-2 to OG in qualifiers and showing map-specific vulnerabilities on Mirage and Nuke. Key factors include Spirit's rested roster post-major break versus PARIVISION's fatigue from qualifiers, plus home-crowd edge in Rotterdam tilting odds further; upsets remain possible if PARIVISION exploits Vertigo prowess, but historical head-to-heads (Spirit 2-0 aggregate) underpin the pricing.

question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (HIGH) $115 by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or above the listed price between market creation and the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a modest 45% implied probability for WTI Crude Oil (CL) surpassing $85 by June 30, reflecting caution amid softening demand signals despite recent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East boosting prices to $82/bbl. Key drivers include a surprise 3.6M bbl EIA inventory draw last week, countering high stockpiles, but China's tepid economic rebound caps upside. Summer driving season could provide tailwinds, yet Fed rate cut expectations (post-June FOMC) risk weakening the USD further. Watch Thursday's EIA report and OPEC+ compliance updates; a sustained break above $83 resistance tilts odds higher in this capital-backed sentiment gauge.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 21, 1PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Set 1 Winner: Jovic vs Badosa
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Iva Jovic and Paula Badosa in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to “Jovic” if Iva Jovic wins the first set. It will resolve to “Badosa” if Paula Badosa wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA match statistics.
groupItemTitle: Miami Open: Iva Jovic vs Paula Badosa Set 1 Winner

events.title: Miami Open: Iva Jovic vs Paula Badosa
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Iva Jovic and Paula Badosa in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Iva Jovic' if Iva Jovic advances against Paula Badosa.

This market will resolve to 'Paula Badosa' if Paula Badosa advances against Iva Jovic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iva Jovic's breakout run as a 17-year-old wildcard has traders viewing this Miami Open round-of-16 clash as a coin flip against the more experienced Paula Badosa, with Badosa holding a slim 50.5% implied probability edge. Jovic, ranked No. 998, stunned Putintseva and Brengle on hard courts, showcasing aggressive baseline play and serving under pressure, while Badosa (No. 70) advances steadily post-injury layoff with wins over Kalinskaya and Cocciaretto, leveraging her powerful groundstrokes honed on similar surfaces. No head-to-head exists, but Badosa's superior ranking and 60% win rate in 2024 Miami tune-ups create balance against Jovic's momentum. A Jovic upset could stem from hot serving; Badosa tips scales with tactical depth if she exploits errors. Crowd wisdom reflects uncertainty in this high-variance matchup.

question: Iva Jovic vs. Paula Badosa: Total Sets O/U 2.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Iva Jovic and Paula Badosa in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Naomi Osaka's superior experience and hard-court pedigree anchor her 64.5% implied probability against qualifier Talia Gibson in the Miami Open, where the four-time Grand Slam winner has historically dominated the event with a runner-up finish in 2018. No head-to-head exists, but Osaka's 75% win rate on Miami hard courts versus Gibson's inexperience tilts trader consensus her way, though qualifiers often produce upsets if serves hold. Both players report no injuries per latest reports.

eventMetadata.context_description: Naomi Osaka's superior experience and hard-court pedigree anchor her 64.5% implied probability against qualifier Talia Gibson in the Miami Open, where the four-time Grand Slam winner has historically dominated the event with a runner-up finish in 2018. Osaka enters on momentum from straight-set wins over Karolina Pliskova and Elise Mertens this tournament, showcasing sharpened serve and baseline power post-maternity return, while Gibson, ranked No. 132, grinded through three qualifying matches but lacks WTA main-draw success against top-50 foes. No head-to-head exists, but Osaka's 75% win rate on Miami hard courts versus Gibson's inexperience tilts trader consensus her way, though qualifiers often produce upsets if serves hold. Both players report no injuries per latest reports.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 21, 1PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Will Ruler win MVP at First Stand?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 First Stand Tournament MVP (Most Valuable Player award), currently scheduled for March 16th–March 22nd, 2026.

If this tournament is postponed after April 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, canceled, or an MVP has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If multiple players are declared MVP (e.g., due to ties or special circumstances), this market will resolve in favor of the player whose listed in-game name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the tournament organizer, Riot Games. However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/First_Stand_Tournament/2026) may also be used.

Uncertainties like group draws and form swings keep the market tight.

question: Set Handicap: Osaka (-1.5) vs Gibson (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Naomi Osaka and Talia Gibson in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to "Osaka" if Naomi Osaka wins by 2 or more sets than Talia Gibson, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Gibson."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA results.

events.title: Miami Open: Talia Gibson vs Naomi Osaka
events.description:

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Naomi Osaka's superior experience and hard-court pedigree anchor her 64.5% implied probability against qualifier Talia Gibson in the Miami Open, where the four-time Grand Slam winner has historically dominated the event with a runner-up finish in 2018. Osaka enters on momentum from straight-set wins over Karolina Pliskova and Elise Mertens this tournament, showcasing sharpened serve and baseline power post-maternity return, while Gibson, ranked No. 132, grinded through three qualifying matches but lacks WTA main-draw success against top-50 foes. No head-to-head exists, but Osaka's 75% win rate on Miami hard courts versus Gibson's inexperience tilts trader consensus her way, though qualifiers often produce upsets if serves hold. Both players report no injuries per latest reports.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: INFURITY Gaming vs BIG Academy (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the United21 Playoffs market leans toward INFURITY Gaming over BIG Academy in this best-of-three Counter-Strike 2 matchup, driven by INFURITY's stronger recent form with back-to-back series wins against mid-tier opponents, including a 2-0 sweep last week. BIG Academy, Berlin International Gaming's developmental squad, struggles with inconsistency, dropping three of their last five maps on Ancient and Mirage—common picks here—while posting a 1-4 record in playoffs. No roster changes or stand-ins reported officially, but INFURITY's star AWPer holds a 1.25 rating edge in head-to-heads. Watch for map vetoes favoring INFURITY's T-side aggression; rest advantage to neither as both played yesterday. Upsets remain possible in volatile BO3s.

question: US escorts commercial ship through Hormuz by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States military or federal government officially announces that it will escort, is escorting, or has escorted, any commercial ship through the Strait of Hormuz by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

Escort refers to United States military or government personnel accompanying or actively providing protective overwatch for a specific commercial vessel or convoy during its transit through the Strait of Hormuz. Personnel do not need to be aboard the commercial vessel to qualify. Escort may occur from a separate naval vessel or from aerial assets assigned to accompany or protect the transit.

A qualifying announcement must be definitive. Suggestions, unconfirmed reports, or other non-definitive statements will not count.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that United States military or government personnel have escorted at least one commercial vessel on a journey through the Strait of Hormuz will also be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government and military of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a low probability for the US Navy escorting a commercial vessel through the Strait of Hormuz by the specified deadline, driven by robust US Fifth Fleet patrols from Bahrain that have historically deterred Iranian interference without needing publicized escorts. Recent developments include Iran's April seizure of the MSC Aries near the strait amid Israel-Iran tensions, prompting US warnings but no escalation to direct commercial protection; Houthi disruptions remain focused on the Red Sea. Key factors sustaining calm include daily tanker transits averaging 20 million barrels and diplomatic signaling from Tehran avoiding full Hormuz blockade. Watch for US carrier deployments or Iranian Revolutionary Guard naval drills, which could shift odds.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 21, 3PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Spread: Real Racing Club (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for April 1 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Real Racing Club" if Real Racing Club win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Sporting de Gijón".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Head-to-head history tilts toward Racing (three wins in last five), with promotion race pressure amplifying stakes for both sides.

question: Set Handicap: Vacherot (-1.5) vs Navone (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Valentin Vacherot and Mariano Navone in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to "Vacherot" if Valentin Vacherot wins by 2 or more sets than Mariano Navone, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Navone."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.
groupItemTitle: Miami Open: Mariano Navone vs Valentin Vacherot Set Handicap +/-1.5

events.title: Miami Open: Mariano Navone vs Valentin Vacherot
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Mariano Navone and Valentin Vacherot in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Mariano Navone' if Mariano Navone advances against Valentin Vacherot.

This market will resolve to 'Valentin Vacherot' if Valentin Vacherot advances against Mariano Navone.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Valentin Vacherot's scorching recent form anchors his 74% implied probability against Mariano Navone in the Miami Open first round, highlighted by a Challenger title in Szeged last week and stunning qualifier upsets, including a straight-sets win over top-20 seed Karen Khachanov on these outdoor hard courts. Navone, meanwhile, enters with tepid momentum, posting just a 4-6 record over his last 10 matches and a first-round exit at Indian Wells, where his clay-court strengths have translated unevenly to hard courts. Absent head-to-head history or reported injuries, trader consensus weighs Vacherot's qualifier edge and surface acclimation as pivotal momentum drivers amid the tournament's high-upset potential.

events.title: SD Huesca vs. Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for April 1 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Huesca holds a decisive edge as the higher-division side hosting third-tier Cultural Leonesa in Copa del Rey second-round action, driving trader consensus toward roughly 75% implied probability for Huesca victory or advancement. Official injury reports list no major absences for either, though Huesca's midweek rotation risks could narrow the gap. Leonesa's cup pedigree adds upset potential, but historical Segunda triumphs over inferior foes and home splits favor the hosts amid playoff-irrelevant scheduling.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - March 21, 3PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the ETH/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

events.title: FC Andorra vs. Málaga CF - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for April 1 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Málaga CF enters this Segunda División clash with momentum, unbeaten in six straight matches and riding a three-game winning streak that has traders pricing them as clear favorites against struggling FC Andorra. Andorra, mired in the relegation zone, has lost four of its last five home games, hampered by the absence of key forward Rubén Alcaraz (hamstring strain, per official report). Málaga boasts a perfect head-to-head record over the past two meetings and benefits from rest advantage after no midweek fixture. Recent form and roster edges drive the market's implied probabilities, though Andorra's home crowd could spark an upset in this survival scrap.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 21, 3PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the HYPE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the HYPE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/futures/HYPEUSDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance HYPE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Queens (NC) Royals vs. Purdue Boilermakers: O/U 162.5
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:35 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Queens (NC) Royals and Purdue Boilermakers combine to score 163 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 163, this market will resolve to "Under".

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Queens (NC) Royals vs. Purdue Boilermakers
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Queens (NC) Royals and Purdue Boilermakers on March 20 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Purdue Boilermakers' 97.5% implied probability stems from a stark talent and seeding chasm in this NCAA Tournament first-round clash, pitting the No. 1-seeded Big Ten powerhouse against Queens Royals, a 16th-seeded Division II newcomer making their March Madness debut. Purdue's Zach Edey, the towering two-time National Player of the Year finalist, anchors a squad fresh off a 29-win season with elite rebounding and scoring efficiency, while Queens relies on guard play but lacks the depth and experience against elite competition. No major injuries mar Purdue's official report, and historical 1-vs-16 dominance (35-1 since 1985) bolsters trader consensus. Realistic upset risks include Edey foul trouble, Queens' unheralded shooters erupting for 20+ threes, or Purdue's occasional slow starts snowballing in a neutral-site battle.

question: Spread: Flames (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 20 at 9:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Flames" if the Flames win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Panthers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Panthers vs. Flames
events.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 20 at 9:00PM ET:
If the Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Panthers".
If the Flames win, the market will resolve to "Flames".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers' scorching 6-1-0 start, including a five-game road win streak capped by a 5-3 victory over Edmonton, positions them as heavy favorites against the middling Calgary Flames (3-3-1) in tonight's Saddledome matchup. The defending Stanley Cup champs boast elite goaltending from Sergei Bobrovsky (1.95 GAA) and balanced scoring from Aleksander Barkov and Matthew Tkachuk, while Flames rely on Nazem Kadri's hot hand amid defensive lapses allowing 3.57 goals per game. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Calgary's home-ice edge and recent 3-2 win over Minnesota add upset potential—traders price Panthers at around 65% implied win probability reflecting momentum disparity.

question: Bank of Japan decreases interest rates after the June 2026 meeting?
description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for June is scheduled to be released on June 16, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's June 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward no change in the Bank of Japan's policy rate at 57%, driven by Governor Ueda's recent dovish signals emphasizing data-dependent caution amid yen volatility near 157/USD and softening core inflation trends, with Tokyo CPI easing to 2.3% in May from prior highs. A modest 25 bps hike to 0.25% garners 30.4% implied odds, fueled by robust spring wage gains exceeding 5% and persistent above-target inflation, though global uncertainties like sticky US data temper aggression. Larger 50+ bps moves (17.5%) reflect tail risks from intervention threats, while cuts at 5.5% appear negligible given Japan's accommodative stance post-March normalization; watch June 13-14 meeting for super-long bond yield tweaks as pivotal catalysts.

question: Counter-Strike: UNiTY esports vs G2 Ares (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between UNiTY esports and G2 Ares in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 21 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "UNiTY esports" if UNiTY esports win the match against G2 Ares.

This market will resolve to "G2 Ares" if G2 Ares win the match against UNiTY esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: G2 Ares holds a trader edge in the UNiTY esports BO3 matchup at United21 Playoffs, reflecting their stronger recent form with three straight series wins in qualifiers against tier-2 foes, boasting a 65% map win rate on key maps like Mirage and Inferno. UNiTY, fresh off a roster tweak with AWPer Snax joining, shows inconsistency, dropping 2-1 losses to comparable squads amid pistol round vulnerabilities. Head-to-head favors G2 2-1 this season, but UNiTY's home crowd in playoffs and rest advantage could spark an upset; watch player KD ratios and first-half momentum for live shifts in implied probabilities.

question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between UNiTY esports and G2 Ares in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 21 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if UNiTY esports and G2 Ares play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between UNiTY esports and G2 Ares in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 21 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "UNiTY esports" if UNiTY esports win the match against G2 Ares.

This market will resolve to "G2 Ares" if G2 Ares win the match against UNiTY esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: G2 Ares holds a trader edge in the UNiTY esports BO3 matchup at United21 Playoffs, reflecting their stronger recent form with three straight series wins in qualifiers against tier-2 foes, boasting a 65% map win rate on key maps like Mirage and Inferno. UNiTY, fresh off a roster tweak with AWPer Snax joining, shows inconsistency, dropping 2-1 losses to comparable squads amid pistol round vulnerabilities. Head-to-head favors G2 2-1 this season, but UNiTY's home crowd in playoffs and rest advantage could spark an upset; watch player KD ratios and first-half momentum for live shifts in implied probabilities.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: STATE enters as the clear favorite in this United21 Playoffs BO3 matchup against B8 Academy, with trader consensus implying around 75% win probability reflecting STATE's higher HLTV ranking (top 40) and stronger recent form, including a 2-1 upset over higher seeds in qualifiers. B8 Academy, a developmental squad, struggles with inconsistency, posting a 40% win rate over their last 10 maps against tier-2 foes, hampered by roster inexperience. No major roster changes or bans reported; key watch is STATE AWPer sc0ut's dominance on Mirage and Inferno, where B8 leaks picks. Playoff pressure favors STATE's veterans, though B8's upset potential lingers on Nuke if they steal pistol rounds.

description:

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Will the ECB announce a 50+ bps decrease at the June 2026 meeting?
description:
This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the deposit facility rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the European Central Bank's (ECB) June 2026 meeting.

If the deposit facility rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 basis points and will resolve to the relevant bracket. For example, if the deposit facility rate is increased or decreased by 12.5 basis points, it will be treated as a 25 basis point change for the purposes of resolution.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the European Central Bank after its June 11, 2026 monetary policy meeting, as listed on the official ECB calendar:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/mgcgc/html/index.en.html

The level and change of the deposit facility rate is also published at the official ECB interest rates page:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html

This market may resolve as soon as the ECB releases its interest rate decision following the June 11, 2026, meeting.

If no interest rate decision or update is published by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the “No change” bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket's ECB June 2026 interest rate decision hinges on a razor-thin contest between a 25 basis point hike (50% implied probability) and no change (41%), fueled by sticky Eurozone services inflation at 4.0% and robust wage growth exceeding 4%, which challenge the ECB's 2% target despite headline CPI dipping to 1.8% in October. Recent deposit rate cut to 3.25% signals ongoing easing bias, but forward curves from swaps markets price in potential policy pivot if core measures rebound. Key differentiators include November CPI release on December 2, Q4 GDP data, and Lagarde's December 12 guidance, with historical ECB pauses during wage disinflation delays tilting odds toward status quo if growth softens below 0.8% quarterly.

question: Will the ECB announce a 25 bps increase at the June 2026 meeting?
description:
This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the deposit facility rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the European Central Bank's (ECB) June 2026 meeting.

If the deposit facility rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 basis points and will resolve to the relevant bracket. For example, if the deposit facility rate is increased or decreased by 12.5 basis points, it will be treated as a 25 basis point change for the purposes of resolution.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the European Central Bank after its June 11, 2026 monetary policy meeting, as listed on the official ECB calendar:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/mgcgc/html/index.en.html

The level and change of the deposit facility rate is also published at the official ECB interest rates page:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html

This market may resolve as soon as the ECB releases its interest rate decision following the June 11, 2026, meeting.

If no interest rate decision or update is published by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the “No change” bracket.

question: Will Jean-Philippe Veter be the next mayor of Strasbourg?
description:
The second round municipal election to elect the municipal council of Strasbourg is scheduled to take place on March 22, 2026; the elected councillors will then choose the mayor.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next elected Mayor of Strasbourg.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Catherine Trautmann's lead in trader consensus at 53% stems from her designation as the Socialist Party candidate following a recent primary win, bolstered by her prior tenure as Strasbourg mayor from 2001 to 2008 and strong polling among left-leaning voters amid France's shifting municipal landscape. Incumbent Green mayor Jeanne Barseghian holds 30.5% implied probability, supported by her 2020 victory but challenged by coalition fatigue and urban policy critiques. Jean-Philippe Vetter's 21.5% reflects right-wing consolidation under Les Républicains, gaining from national center-right momentum yet trailing in diverse Strasbourg. Recent opinion surveys and candidate announcements have tightened odds, with the 2026 election vote set for March, leaving room for endorsements or debates to shift dynamics.

question: Will East Bengal FC win on 2026-04-16?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 16, 2026
If East Bengal FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: East Bengal FC vs. Bengaluru FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Thursday, April 16, 2026 between East Bengal FC and Bengaluru FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the East Bengal FC vs. Bengaluru FC clash reflects razor-thin margins at 50.5% across all outcomes, capturing the ISL intensity between two playoff-hungry mid-table sides with identical recent form—each securing one win and one draw in their last two outings. East Bengal's home edge at Salt Lake Stadium pairs with attacking threats like Cleiton Silva, fresh off a goal contribution, while Bengaluru FC counters with a robust away defense (conceding just 1.2 goals per game) and Sunil Chhetri's enduring influence despite no major injuries per official reports. Head-to-head history tilts slightly Bengaluru's way (4-3 in last 10), but mutual inconsistencies and tactical caution often yield stalemates, keeping probabilities bunched.
